## CONSULTATION HANDOFF

```
change_type: Add Feature
feature_name: enterprise-sdlc-phase-1
date: 2026-05-07
advisor: [@devstarter-techlead, @devstarter-pm]
```

---

### Section 1 — Problem / Request

**What the user described:**
```
DevStarter should match enterprise SDLC capability — like a big company
with a full team (tech lead, senior dev, BA, SA, PM, QA, tester, etc.).
What's missing? What roles and workflows should be added?
```

**Change type detected:**
- [x] Add Feature — new agents and workflows that don't exist yet
- [ ] Modify Feature
- [ ] Fix Bug

---

### Section 2 — Analysis Summary

**Situation understood:**
```
DevStarter today = 13 agents + 41 skills + 28 SDLC runbooks.
Strong on solo/small-team velocity. Enterprise gaps come in three
categories: missing roles (SA, SRE, RM, PO, Scrum Master, Test Eng,
Perf Eng, Eng Mgr); missing workflows (ADR, RCA, DoR, tech debt
register, SLO/SLA, CAB, performance testing, etc.); and missing
governance (compliance audit, ARB, vuln mgmt SLA).
```

**Gap (for features):**
```
DevStarter assumes one builder wears many hats. Enterprise teams
specialize — and rituals/gates exist BECAUSE of that specialization.
The biggest immediate wins are workflows that make existing implicit
responsibilities explicit (e.g., TechLead "owns ADRs" but there's
no ADR runbook).
```

**Recommended Phase 1 scope (5 additions):**
```
1. ADR workflow                 — sdlc/devstarter-adr.md + skill + intake
2. Post-mortem / RCA workflow   — sdlc/devstarter-rca.md + skill + template
3. SA agent (System Analyst)    — agents/devstarter-sa.md + skill
4. Definition of Ready (DoR)    — sdlc/devstarter-dor.md + integrate w/ sprint
5. Tech debt register workflow  — sdlc/devstarter-techdebt.md + skill + template
```

---

### Section 3 — Recommended Approach

**Recommended option:**
```
Phase 1 only — add 5 highest-impact gaps. Defer Phase 2 (SRE, RM,
performance testing, etc.) and Phase 3 (PO, Eng Mgr, compliance,
A/B testing) to later releases.
```

**Why this was recommended:**
```
Phase 1 picks gaps that are (a) implicitly assumed in existing flows,
(b) standalone (don't require deep refactoring), (c) high leverage
(used in every project). Each addition is ~1 SDLC runbook + 1
SKILL.md + optional 1 intake template. Roughly a single-sprint scope
for a meaningful enterprise upgrade.
```

**Estimated effort:** L (8–24h for all 5 items)

**Risks or caveats:**
```
- Risk of agent/workflow proliferation — each new agent must have a
  clearly distinct charter from existing agents (don't duplicate)
- SA vs BA boundary needs explicit definition to avoid overlap
- ADR workflow needs to integrate cleanly with existing /devstarter-techlead
- Tech debt register needs to integrate with /devstarter-audit output
```

---

### Section 4 — Acceptance Criteria

**Done when:**
```
Given Phase 1 is implemented
When the user runs /devstarter-menu
Then 5 new commands appear: /devstarter-adr, /devstarter-rca,
     /devstarter-sa, /devstarter-dor, /devstarter-techdebt
And  agents/devstarter-sa.md exists with clear BA boundary
And  each new SDLC has Model: header + intake template if applicable
And  CLAUDE.md is updated with new agent in role table
And  Recently Shipped log includes the new release version
```

**Regression guard:**
```
- All 13 existing agents remain unchanged
- All 41 existing skills remain unchanged
- /devstarter-menu still loads correctly
- /devstarter-techlead still references ADR ownership (now points to /adr)
- /devstarter-incident still works (now references /rca for follow-up)
```
